proposalCandidates.title: Refresh retro structural-cause follow-up scenario from recent activity
proposalCandidates.name: Retro Structural Cause Follow-up
proposalCandidates.description: The user keeps pushing past a local explanation until the assistant generalizes the structural cause and proposes a durable retro change.
proposalCandidates.brief: Recent activity shows repeated correction pressure before the assistant lands on a structural retro.
simulatorTurns: 이 패턴이 발생할 수밖에 없었던 구조적 원인을 더 봐주세요.
리트로 스킬도 그 관점으로 바뀌어야 하지 않나요?

evidence.title: retro structural cause follow-up

proposalCandidates.title: Refresh quality proof-layering follow-up scenario from recent activity
proposalCandidates.name: Quality Proof Layering Follow-up
proposalCandidates.description: The user refines the distinction between public executable specs, on-demand proof, and the specdown viewer until the assistant aligns the layering policy correctly.
proposalCandidates.brief: Recent activity shows a multi-turn quality discussion that depends on remembering the specdown SoT versus viewer split.
simulatorTurns: 스펙다운은 SoT와 viewer 두 역할이 있다고 봅니다.
source guard는 전자 때문에 줄이고 싶지만, 후자 때문에 결과는 보여야 해요.
이 관점이 quality skill에도 반영돼 있나요?

evidence.title: quality proof layering follow-up

proposalCandidates.title: Refresh premortem canonical-subagent follow-up scenario from recent activity
proposalCandidates.name: Premortem Canonical Subagent Follow-up
proposalCandidates.description: The user clarifies that premortem should stay on the canonical subagent path, and the assistant must preserve that policy rather than collapsing to same-agent fallback logic.
proposalCandidates.brief: Recent activity shows a policy clarification that only makes sense if the assistant remembers the prior premortem discussion.
simulatorTurns: 이거랑 지금 얘기한 standing eval 범위가 관련 있나요?
정책을 흐리지 말고 구분해서 설명해 주세요.

evidence.title: premortem canonical subagent follow-up
messages: 이거랑 지금 얘기한 standing eval 범위가 관련 있나요?

proposalCandidates.title: Refresh find-skills canonical-artifact follow-up scenario from recent activity
proposalCandidates.name: Find Skills Canonical Artifact Follow-up
proposalCandidates.description: The user keeps pointing out dirty `find-skills` artifacts until the assistant distinguishes canonical durable inventory from ad hoc recommendation output.
proposalCandidates.brief: Recent activity shows repeated correction pressure about artifact churn caused by invocation-shape differences.
simulatorTurns: 이게 충분히 유의미한 정보라면 무조건 커밋해야 하는데 왜 물어보는가?
평소와 다른 방식으로 호출했다는 건 뭐죠?
이건 canonical artifact와 ad hoc query output을 구분해야 하는 문제 아닌가요?
